verdict.note: Orthogonal. SpecSearch.jsx changed ONLY by the STATUS_DOT to STATUS_COLOR rename: the search-row session dot still colours by status from the same single map, no behaviour change. ISSUE/SESSION planes NOT shown — 0 live sessions in harness, and no issue title matched the query. Plane-tagging mechanism verified for 2 of 4 planes; full 4-plane span needs a live session.

A/repro: real Chromium on worktree vite (:5288 → live :8787). Focused .hud-help (document.activeElement confirmed), pressed Enter → legend:false, node-info popup opened instead (infoOverlay:true) — the capture-phase keydown resolves Enter as board.info and preventDefault cancels the button's native Enter→click activation. Mouse click on the same button opens the legend fine. Root cause predates tonight's icon/tooltip pass: 70d1fa44 (07-04) bound Enter to board.info with no focused-control guard; the tooltip commit only swapped title=→data-tip. Screenshot: tooltip shows focus on '?' while the WRONG surface (info popup) is open.

B/pass at ce9c2f38: same scenario, same real-Chromium loop (worktree vite :5288 → live :8787). Focused .hud-help, Enter → legend:true, infoOverlay:false — activation now equals click (Space verified too). Regression check: plain Enter with no control focused still opens the node-info popup and the route stays #/graph (enter-opens-info-not-session-board intact). Fix: capture handler passes Enter/Space through when DOM focus sits on a native control.
Enter with node focused opened the info popup (.ov-backdrop=1) and route stayed on graph (hash never #/sessions); consoleErrors=0
Enter inside the open info popup: popup stays open, hash unchanged (graph), no Session Board navigation; consoleErrors=0
click spec-dashboard: viewport transform unchanged (translate(0,0) scale(.85)); ArrowDown panned to translate(317,338.65); camera follows keyboard not mouse
capture probe: Ctrl+L, Ctrl+',', Alt+Left, Ctrl+0 all defaultPrevented=false; route/focus/overlays unchanged; Ctrl+/ opened the search palette

Real-Chromium YATU drive of the worktree dashboard (vite :5217 → spex serve :8917): popup open on spec-cli, Shift+j → spec-dashboard → spec-forge, Shift+k back, pane 2 (history) persists across the lens move, plain j scrolls (scrollTop 342) without moving focus, Esc closes, bare-board Shift+j = plain sibling nav. 9/9 DOM assertions; whole run on video.

Lens follows focus proven end-to-end via headless-Chromium CDP against a live v0.3.0 backend (/api/graph): popup opens on spec-cli, Shift+Down walks to spec-dashboard then spec-eval with the popup re-rendering each sibling, Shift+Up returns, Shift+Left to parent spexcode, Shift+Right back to child; plain j then scrolls the pane 0->716 with focus unchanged. Unmodified keys keep popup meaning, Shift+nav does the relationship walk.

Real DOM focus on the HUD '?' button (activeElement=hud-help); Enter reaches it with defaultPrevented=false (board's Enter-as-info alias steps aside) and the native activation fires — the keymap/legend modal opens, route stays #/, no node-info popup. Mouse click and Space open the same modal. Measured via CDP against live v0.3.0 backend.
From the graph with a node focused, Enter opens the node-info popup (.ov-body present) and the route stays #/ (no crossing to #/sessions) — Enter is a plain alias for i.
With the node-info popup open (i), pressing Enter is inert: popup stays open and route stays #/ — no crossing into a session. Pure reading surface.

At fresh load (mouse mode), clicking a non-focused node (spexcode->spec-dashboard) changes focus but the viewport transform is unchanged (translate 0,0 both before and after) — no pan. A subsequent j keypress DOES pan (translate 0,0 -> 317,345). Camera follows the keyboard, not the mouse.

Live v0.3.0 /api/graph.

Reproduced through the real /p/<id>/ gateway: (a) clicking the rail project chip leaves DOM focus on it; with the node-info popup open, j/k are swallowed (pane scrollTop 0->0 with 228px overflow) because the overlay guard matches the chip's aria-haspopup=menu although it is OUTSIDE the popup; (b) with focus parked on body, opening+closing the popup RESURRECTS the chip as the focus-return ticket — focus jumps to the top-left chip on its own, killing the next popup's keys. Control probe with body focus scrolls fine (0->198).

Same gateway run, post-fix: chip click leaves activeElement=body (menu still opens; rail links still navigate; fresh-Tab reaches the chip and Enter opens its menu); popup close no longer resurrects the chip (activeElement stays body); j/k scroll the popup pane in the direct-chip case (0->198), ticket case (0->209), and control (0->198). Guard now scoped to controls inside [data-focus-overlay]; rail presses inert via inertChromePress, whose SVG-target gutter false-positive is also fixed.
Re-measure after the overlay-guard scoping in this branch: DOM focus on the HUD ? button, Enter opens the help legend (control activation), info popup does NOT open. Real gateway browser run.

Re-measure post guard-scoping: Enter inside the open popup does nothing — popup stays open, hash stays #/graph.

Re-measure post guard-scoping: with the popup open on side-nav, Shift+J walks to sibling (tooltip), Shift+K back, Shift+H to parent (dashboard-ui) — popup stays open and re-renders onto each focus; plain j does not move focus and keeps its popup meaning (pane scroll measured 0->214 on the same build's side-nav pane in the rail-chip B run).
